info.title: MFS
info.description: Multi-source File-like Search — HTTP /v1 control plane.

post.description:
Zero-billing pre-flight estimate: object/chunk/token counts via
metadata + a local chunker/tokenizer dry-run. No embedding API calls.

CS upload flow: POST a tar(.gz) of a tree as the raw body (?name=<label>);
the server stages + indexes it. For client/server without a shared filesystem.

Manifest-diff upload step ②: stat-only manifest in, need_sha1 + deletion
candidates out. No bytes transferred here.

put.description:
Manifest-diff upload step ④: PUT a tar(.gz) carrying a `.mfs-meta.json`
member (hashes/renames/deletions) + the changed file bytes. The server applies
it to the staging area and triggers the file-connector sync. full=true
(--force-index/--force-upload) forces a re-index of the whole staged tree.

get.description:
Full object content for `mfs export`. Honest about completeness:
each connector's own row cap still applies (postgres `max_read_rows`,
BigQuery `max_read_rows`, etc.), so structured objects above that
threshold return `partial=true`. The bare-cat size guard
(object_too_large_for_cat) does NOT apply — export is the escape
hatch for that — but true streaming export is still TODO.

Unauthenticated liveness/readiness probe (no sensitive data); used by the
compose healthcheck and Helm probes so they work even with auth enabled.

target.title: Target
target.description: path or connector URI to register + index

config.title: Config
config.description: connector config ([[objects]], schemas, _credential_ref, ...); the CLI loads this from --config <file.toml>

since.title: Since
since.description: only index changes since this cursor/date

process.title: Process
process.description: False (default): enqueue and return the job_id immediately (poll GET /v1/jobs/{id}); True: index inline and return when done

update.title: Update
update.description: apply config to an existing connector (connector update); add ignores it

chunk_count.title: Chunk Count
chunk_count.description: indexed vector chunks for this connector

sampled_objects.title: Sampled Objects
sampled_objects.description: objects actually sampled for the dry-run

est_tokens.title: Est Tokens
est_tokens.description: extrapolated token count; apply your provider rate for $

locator.title: Locator
locator.description: per-hit identity. text/code line hits: {'lines':[n,n]}; structured pushdown: connector PK dict; notice rows: null.

path.title: Path
path.description: full object URI — feed to cat/search

search_status.title: Search Status
search_status.description: indexed | partial | not_indexed | null if unseen

indexable.title: Indexable
indexable.description: whether this object is eligible for indexing

path.title: Path
path.description: path relative to the upload root

client_id.title: Client Id
client_id.description: stable client id (from client.toml) — connector identity

root.title: Root
root.description: client absolute path of the upload root (identity + scope)

need_sha1.title: Need Sha1
need_sha1.description: paths whose bytes the server needs

deletion_candidates.title: Deletion Candidates
deletion_candidates.description: server-known paths absent from the manifest (for rename pairing)

locator.title: Locator
locator.description: per-chunk identity. body/code/document chunks: {'lines':[start,end]}; structured (DB row, issue, slack thread): connector PK dict (e.g. {'id':1}, {'number':42}, {'thread_ts':'...'}); once-per-object kinds: null.

ResultEnvelope.title: ResultEnvelope
ResultEnvelope.description:
One search/grep hit. Outer shape is stable across connectors;
locator + metadata.fields are per-connector but documented.
